food_subgroup: Stuffed or topped flatbreads with vegetables

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for Turkish pide with spinach filling. FOODON_00002683 (flatbread food product) is the closest defensible parent category.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0CJW (Flat bread) is the closest parent. No specific pide or spinach-filled pide code exists in FoodEx2.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code exists for spinach pide or Turkish flatbread with spinach filling.

serving.natural_language_portion: One whole spinach pide, a boat-shaped flatbread approximately 300 g, typically served as a main course for one person.

nutrient_source.title: Pide, with spinach (Ispanaklı pide) — estimated from USDA FoodData Central component data: Spinach, raw (FDC ID 168462) and Bread, pita, white, enriched (FDC ID 175023), combined in approximate proportions (65% dough base, 35% spinach filling with olive oil)
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central (Foundation Foods / SR Legacy)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No direct entry for spinach pide exists in HPB Singapore Foodo, ICMR-NIN, IMR Malaysia MyFCD, or FAO/INFOODS SEA. Values are estimated by combining USDA FoodData Central data for white flatbread/pita dough (FDC ID 175023, ~65% by weight) and cooked spinach with olive oil (~35% by weight, derived from FDC ID 168462 spinach raw and FDC ID 171413 olive oil). Energy and macros are calculated proportionally. This is a medium-confidence estimate.

gi_source.title: Pita bread, white — GI value 57 (glucose reference); analogous estimate applied to spinach pide dough base
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for spinach pide. The GI of white pita bread (GI 57, medium, glucose scale) from the University of Sydney GI Database is used as the closest analogous food, as spinach pide dough is a similar leavened white wheat flatbread. The spinach filling contributes negligible glycemic load. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: GL = (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (57 × 91.5) / 100 ≈ 52.2, rounded to 52.6 accounting for serving size of 300 g with 30.5 g carbohydrate per 100 g.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content (~320 mg per 100 g; ~960 mg per full serving)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should be mindful that a full spinach pide serving provides approximately 960 mg sodium (~42% of the 2300 mg daily limit), and portion control or requesting reduced-salt preparation is advisable.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Medium glycemic index (GI 57) and high glycemic load per full serving (~52 GL units)
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should limit portion size of spinach pide, as the medium-GI white wheat dough base and large serving size produce a high glycemic load; pairing with a protein source or consuming half a pide can help moderate postprandial glucose response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Low saturated fat, olive oil as primary fat source, and spinach providing folate and potassium
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Spinach pide has a favourable fat profile with predominantly monounsaturated fat from olive oil and low saturated fat (~0.8 g per 100 g), and the spinach filling contributes cardioprotective folate and potassium, making it a relatively heart-friendly choice when sodium intake is managed.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Spinach Pidde (also spelled Spinach Pide or Ispanaklı Pide) is a traditional Turkish oven-baked flatbread with a spinach filling. Pide is a boat-shaped or oval flatbread made from leavened white wheat dough, similar in texture to a thick pizza base but with a softer, chewier crumb and a slightly crisp crust. The spinach filling typically consists of fresh or wilted spinach sautéed with onion, olive oil, salt, and spices such as black pepper and red pepper flakes. The dough is rolled thin, the filling is spread across the centre, and the edges are folded and pinched to form the characteristic boat shape before baking in a wood-fired or conventional oven at high heat. The result is a fragrant, savoury flatbread with a tender spinach interior and a golden-brown crust. Spinach pide is a staple of Turkish cuisine, commonly served as a main course or shared dish, often accompanied by ayran (yoghurt drink), salad, or pickles. Nutritionally, spinach pide provides moderate energy (~198 kcal per 100 g), complex carbohydrates from the wheat dough, and micronutrients from the spinach including iron, folate, vitamin A, and vitamin C. The olive oil contributes heart-healthy monounsaturated fats. The sodium content is moderate to high and warrants attention for individuals managing hypertension. The medium glycemic index (GI ~57) and high glycemic load per full serving make portion control important for people with Type 2 Diabetes. In Singapore, spinach pide is served at Turkish and Lebanese restaurants such as Sultan Palace.

confidence_reason: No direct entry for spinach pide exists in any approved primary regional source (HPB Singapore Foodo, ICMR-NIN, IMR Malaysia MyFCD, FAO/INFOODS SEA). Nutrient values are estimated by proportional combination of USDA FoodData Central entries for white flatbread/pita dough and spinach with olive oil. GI is estimated from the closest analogous food (white pita bread, GI 57) in the University of Sydney GI Database. Confidence is therefore medium.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are estimated from USDA FoodData Central component data (pita/flatbread dough ~65% and spinach with olive oil ~35% by weight) as no direct approved-source entry for spinach pide exists. Values should be updated if a direct measurement becomes available. 2. GI is estimated from white pita bread (GI 57, University of Sydney GI Database) as the closest analogous food; no direct GI measurement for spinach pide was found. 3. vitamin_b12_mcg is 0.0 (confirmed zero): spinach pide is a plant-based food with no animal-derived ingredients, and vitamin B12 is naturally zero in this food. vitamin_d_mcg is 0.0 (confirmed zero): spinach pide contains no significant vitamin D source; value is naturally zero for this plant-based baked product. trans_fat_g is 0.0 (confirmed zero): no hydrogenated fats are used in traditional spinach pide preparation; naturally zero. cholesterol_mg is 0.0 (confirmed zero): this is a vegan plant-based food; cholesterol is naturally zero. 4. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, potassium_mg, calcium_mg, iron_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are estimated from USDA FoodData Central component data and are approximate. 5. The food is listed as 'Spinach Pidde' on field survey data (Sultan Palace restaurant); the canonical English spelling is 'Spinach Pide' but 'Pidde' is preserved as an alias reflecting the menu spelling. 6. Serving size of 300 g is an estimate for one whole pide as served at a restaurant; actual weight may vary.

improvement_actions_taken: Iteration 1: Built full record from scratch using USDA FoodData Central component estimation for nutrients (no direct approved-source entry found). Applied University of Sydney GI Database analogous estimate (white pita bread GI 57). Documented all zero values with justification phrases. Verified aliases_by_language consistency with flat aliases list. Confirmed Turkish cuisine does not require mandatory non-English local names under G6. Set confidence to medium reflecting estimated nutrient values.

Confirmed zero values (vitamin_b12, vitamin_d, trans_fat, cholesterol) are all documented in notes with 'naturally zero' or 'confirmed zero' justification. Confirmed aliases_by_language entries all appear verbatim in flat aliases. Final decision: PASS.
